channel.title: Web3 Security Paper Digest
channel.description: Curated papers on Web3, blockchain, and smart contract security from ArXiv and ACL

item.title: SymGPT: Auditing Smart Contracts via Combining Symbolic Execution with Large Language Models
item.description:
Summary

  • Introduces SymGPT, a tool that combines large language models with symbolic execution to verify smart contracts' compliance with ERC standards.
  • Performs an empirical analysis of 132 ERC rules, translates natural-language ERC rules into a domain-specific language via an LLM, synthesizes constraints, and uses symbolic execution to detect violations and generate attack paths.
  • Evaluated on 4,000 real-world contracts, identifying 5,783 ERC rule violations including 1,375 violations with clear attack paths for financial theft, and outperforming six automated techniques and a security-expert auditing service.

Paper Type

  • Research

Additional Information

  • Authors: ['Shihao Xia', 'Mengting He', 'Shuai Shao', 'Tingting Yu', 'Yiying Zhang', 'Nobuko Yoshida', 'Linhai Song']
  • Tags: ['smart-contracts', 'symbolic-execution', 'large-language-models', 'ERC-compliance', 'vulnerability-detection']
